stages.name: git-init
stages.command: git config --global --add safe.directory /workspace/project && cd /workspace/project && TAG=$(date +%b%d-%H%M | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]') && git checkout -b autoresearch/$TAG && echo "Created branch autoresearch/$TAG" && echo '[STAGE_COMPLETE]'

stages.name: build
stages.prompt:
You are an autonomous ML researcher. Your goal is to minimize val_bpb by modifying train.py.

1. Read /workspace/project/src/train.py for context.
2. Read /workspace/results/results.tsv (if it exists) to see past experiments.
3. Choose an experiment idea — change architecture, optimizer, hyperparameters, batch size, model size, etc.
4. Modify /workspace/project/src/train.py with your changes.
5. Write a one-line description of what you changed to /workspace/msg/commit-msg.txt

Rules:
- This is a CPU-only project. There is NO GPU available. Do NOT use torch.cuda, CUDA devices, or any GPU-specific code (flash attention, kernels, etc.). All tensors must stay on CPU.
- Only modify src/train.py. Do NOT modify src/prepare.py or any other file.
- Do NOT install new packages.
- Do NOT run train.py or any Python script. You are the build stage — code modification only. Testing is handled by a separate stage.
- Keep changes focused — one idea per iteration.
- If you see repeated failures in results.tsv, try a different direction.

When done, emit [STAGE_COMPLETE].

transitions.prompt: Code modification complete, commit message written

stages.name: git-commit
stages.command: git config --global --add safe.directory /workspace/project && cd /workspace/project && git add -A && (git diff --cached --quiet && echo 'Nothing to commit' || git commit -m "$(cat /workspace/msg/commit-msg.txt 2>/dev/null || echo 'experiment iteration')") && echo '[STAGE_COMPLETE]'

stages.name: test
stages.command: mkdir -p /workspace/cache && ln -sfn /workspace/cache /root/.cache && cd /workspace/project && UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT=/workspace/venv uv run src/prepare.py && UV_PROJECT_ENVIRONMENT=/workspace/venv uv run src/train.py > /workspace/results/run.log 2>&1; grep '^val_bpb:\|^peak_rss_mb:' /workspace/results/run.log > /workspace/results/metrics.txt 2>/dev/null; echo '[STAGE_COMPLETE]'

stages.name: review
stages.prompt:
You are reviewing the results of an ML experiment.

1. Read /workspace/results/metrics.txt for the key metrics (val_bpb, peak_rss_mb).
2. If metrics.txt is empty, the run crashed. Read the last 50 lines of /workspace/results/run.log to understand why.
3. Read /workspace/results/results.tsv to compare with previous experiments.
4. Decide:
   - If val_bpb improved (lower than best previous): emit [STAGE_KEEP]
   - If val_bpb is worse: emit [STAGE_RESET]
   - If the run crashed: emit [STAGE_RESET]
5. Append a row to /workspace/results/results.tsv:
   commit\tval_bpb\tmemory_gb\tstatus\tdescription
   - Use `git rev-parse --short HEAD` for the commit hash
   - status: keep, discard, or crash

IMPORTANT: Always emit exactly one marker: [STAGE_KEEP] or [STAGE_RESET].

transitions.prompt: Experiment improved val_bpb — keep the commit
Experiment did not improve — revert the commit

stages.name: git-reset
stages.command: git config --global --add safe.directory /workspace/project && cd /workspace/project && git reset --hard HEAD~1 && echo '[STAGE_COMPLETE]'
